beats.text: This area in the Persian Gulf was quiet through the previous day.
At 01:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 98% jamming probability for 13:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
13:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
This area in the Persian Gulf was quiet through the previous day.
At 07:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 97% jamming probability for 19:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
19:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
This area in eastern Europe was quiet through the previous day.
At 08:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 78% jamming probability for 20:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
20:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
The record keeps its failures too. This point sits in the Black Sea.
At 00:00 UTC the interference channel lit up here. The 12-hours-ahead forecast had put the risk at just 1%.
Interference appeared here without a high-risk flag. It stays on the record — misses are published, never hidden.
